claim: IPCC AR6 identifies South Asian cities as high-risk for pluvial flooding with impervious-surface expansion outpacing drainage capacity by 2-3x and rainfall intensity increasing with warming.

source.title: IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 6: Cities, Settlements and Key Infrastructure

method: Reviewed urban flooding section, impervious-surface trends, and drainage-capacity analysis.
limitations: City-scale projections are modeled, not observed for all cities.

claim: A 2021 Nature study demonstrated that satellite imagery and cloud-penetrating SAR can map flood extent at 10m resolution globally, with validation showing 85 percent accuracy against ground observations.

source.title: Satellite imaging reveals increased proportion of population exposed to floods

method: Reviewed flood-mapping methodology, accuracy assessment, and population-exposure analysis.
limitations: SAR flood detection is less reliable in urban canyons and under dense vegetation.
